canonical_definition: A systematic difference in model behavior across socially or factually contrastive conditions, reflecting either social bias or susceptibility to misinformation, which persists as a latent trait across evaluation instances.
papers: Large Language Models Meet Knowledge Graphs for Question Answering: Synthesis and Opportunities
